question: [File: 'File Name: rest/controllers/cryptoAllowanceController.js -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: getAccountCryptoAllowances()] Can an unprivileged external user query /accounts/:idOrAliasOrEvmAddress/allowances/crypto for any arbitrary account ID to enumerate all active spenders and their allowance amounts, enabling them to identify accounts with large HBAR balances (and thus large unpaid staking rewards) that have granted allowances to third parties, allowing targeted theft of those staking rewards before they are claimed?

[File: 'File Name: rest/controllers/cryptoAllowanceController.js -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: getAccountCryptoAllowances()] Can an unprivileged external user resolve a victim account via EVM address through EntityService.getEncodedId() to discover active crypto allowances on that account, then use those allowances off-chain to drain the account's HBAR balance before unpaid staking rewards are distributed, causing permanent loss of staking rewards?

[File: 'File Name: rest/controllers/cryptoAllowanceController.js -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: extractCryptoAllowancesQuery()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that CryptoAllowanceController.amountCondition ('amount > 0') is appended last in the conditions array after spender filters, allowing a crafted spender.id filter to interact with the condition ordering in a way that bypasses the amount > 0 guard and reveals zero-amount (revoked) allowances, leaking staking reward account relationships?

[File: 'File Name: rest/controllers/cryptoAllowanceController.js -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: extractCryptoAllowancesQuery()] Can an unprivileged external user supply multiple spender.id=eq: parameters to cause updateConditionsAndParamsWithInValues() to accumulate values in spenderInValues[], then call updateQueryFiltersWithInValues() with a misaligned parameter position (conditions.length + 1 vs actual params length), producing a SQL query that skips the amountCondition check and exposes allowances on staking reward accounts?

[File: 'File Name: rest/controllers/cryptoAllowanceController.js -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: getAccountCryptoAllowances()] Can an unprivileged external user pass an account alias string to EntityService.getEncodedId() that resolves to a staking reward distribution account (account 0.0.800), then enumerate all crypto allowances granted by that account, revealing which spenders can drain staking reward funds before they are distributed to stakers?

[File: 'File Name: rest/controllers/cryptoAllowanceController.js -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: getAccountCryptoAllowances()] Can an unprivileged external user use the pagination mechanism (response.links.next built from lastRow.spender) to perform a full enumeration of all spenders with active allowances on a high-value staking account, enabling the attacker to identify the spender with the largest allowance and use it to steal unpaid staking rewards?

[File: 'File Name: rest/controllers/cryptoAllowanceController.js -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: extractCryptoAllowancesQuery()] Can an unprivileged external user supply a spender.id filter with operator 'gt' or 'lt' to cause updateConditionsAndParamsWithInValues() to push a raw condition string (fullName + filter.operator + '$' + position) where the position index is calculated from conditions.length + 1 rather than params.length + 1, creating a parameter index mismatch that causes the query to bind the wrong value and bypass the amount > 0 filter on staking reward accounts?

[File: 'File Name: rest/controllers/cryptoAllowanceController.js -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: getAccountCryptoAllowances()] Can an unprivileged external user query the allowances endpoint with a limit parameter set to the maximum allowed value to bulk-harvest all active crypto allowances across a staking reward account in a single request, then use the disclosed spender list to identify which accounts can drain staking rewards before the next reward distribution?

[File: 'File Name: rest/controllers/cryptoAllowanceController.js -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: getAccountCryptoAllowances()] Can an unprivileged external user pass an EVM address that maps to a long-zero Hedera account ID (NUM_ALIAS type) for a known staking node account through EntityService.getEncodedId(), enumerate its allowances, and use the disclosed spender relationships to front-run staking reward claims?

[File: 'File Name: rest/controllers/cryptoAllowanceController.js -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: extractCryptoAllowancesQuery()] Can an unprivileged external user send a request with no spender.id filter so that spenderInValues remains empty and updateQueryFiltersWithInValues() adds no spender condition, causing the query to return ALL allowances for the target account including those on staking reward accounts, leaking the full set of spenders who could drain unpaid rewards?

[File: 'File Name: rest/controllers/cryptoAllowanceController.js -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: getAccountCryptoAllowances()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the CryptoAllowanceViewModel (via BaseAllowanceViewModel) exposing amount, amount_granted, owner, spender, and timestamp fields to calculate the difference between amount_granted and amount, inferring how much HBAR has already been spent from an allowance and estimating the remaining balance available to drain including unpaid staking rewards?

[File: 'File Name: rest/controllers/cryptoAllowanceController.js -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: getAccountCryptoAllowances()] Can an unprivileged external user use the timestamp.from field exposed by BaseAllowanceViewModel (via nsToSecNs) to determine exactly when a crypto allowance was granted on a staking account, correlating this with known staking reward distribution periods to time an attack that drains the account just before rewards are paid out?

[File: 'File Name: rest/controllers/cryptoAllowanceController.js -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: extractCryptoAllowancesQuery()] Can an unprivileged external user supply a spender.id with the 'gte' operator to cause updateConditionsAndParamsWithInValues() to push a condition with position calculated as conditions.length + 1 (which equals 2 at that point), but since accountId is already at $1 and no other params exist yet, the position correctly maps to $2 — however, if multiple non-eq spender filters are chained, does the position counter drift, causing a wrong param binding that skips the amountCondition and exposes zero-balance staking reward allowances?

[File: 'File Name: rest/controllers/cryptoAllowanceController.js -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: getAccountCryptoAllowances()] Can an unprivileged external user enumerate allowances for a victim account using order=asc combined with spender.id=gte:0 to walk through all spenders in ascending order, building a complete map of all accounts authorized to spend HBAR from a staking reward account, enabling targeted theft of unpaid rewards?

[File: 'File Name: rest/controllers/cryptoAllowanceController.js -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: getAccountCryptoAllowances()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that CryptoAllowanceService.getAccountCryptoAllowances() uses 'select *' from the crypto_allowance table, potentially returning undocumented columns (such as payer_account_id) that reveal additional account relationships on staking reward accounts, enabling the attacker to identify alternative paths to drain unpaid staking rewards?

[File: 'File Name: rest/controllers/cryptoAllowanceController.js -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: getAccountCryptoAllowances()] Can an unprivileged external user pass an account alias that resolves to a deleted account (where EntityService.entityFromAliasQuery filters coalesce(deleted, false) <> true) but the underlying staking reward balance is still pending, allowing the attacker to enumerate allowances on a 'ghost' account and use them to claim unpaid staking rewards before the account is fully purged?

[File: 'File Name: rest/controllers/cryptoAllowanceController.js -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: extractCryptoAllowancesQuery()] Can an unprivileged external user send a request with order=asc and a high limit to retrieve allowances sorted by spender in ascending order, then use the pagination link (which embeds the last spender ID) to perform cursor-based enumeration of all spenders on a staking reward account without triggering rate limits, ultimately identifying the spender with the largest allowance to steal unpaid rewards?

[File: 'File Name: rest/controllers/cryptoAllowanceController.js -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: getAccountCryptoAllowances()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the lack of authentication on the /accounts/:idOrAliasOrEvmAddress/allowances/crypto endpoint to query allowances for any account ID in the range of known staking node accounts (0.0.3 through 0.0.28), discovering which accounts have granted HBAR allowances that could be used to drain staking rewards before distribution?

[File: 'File Name: rest/controllers/cryptoAllowanceController.js -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: getAccountCryptoAllowances()] Can an unprivileged external user use the amount field returned by CryptoAllowanceViewModel to identify allowances where amount equals amount_granted (meaning no HBAR has been spent yet), targeting those accounts for theft of their full HBAR balance including unpaid staking rewards?

[File: 'File Name: rest/controllers/cryptoAllowanceController.js -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: extractCryptoAllowancesQuery()] Can an unprivileged external user supply multiple spender.id=eq: values (aggregated into spenderInValues[]) combined with a spender.id=gt: value (pushed directly to params), causing updateQueryFiltersWithInValues() to calculate start = existingParams.length + 1 incorrectly because the gt condition already pushed a value, resulting in a parameter index collision that corrupts the WHERE clause and bypasses the amount > 0 filter on staking reward accounts?

[File: 'File Name: rest/controllers/cryptoAllowanceController.js -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: getAccountCryptoAllowances()] Can an unprivileged external user pass a shard.realm.num formatted account ID (e.g., 0.0.800) directly to EntityService.getEncodedId() to query allowances on the Hedera staking reward account, enumerating all spenders authorized to transfer HBAR from that account and enabling theft of unpaid staking rewards?

[File: 'File Name: rest/controllers/cryptoAllowanceController.js -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: getAccountCryptoAllowances()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the CryptoAllowance model's use of lodash mapKeys with camelCase to discover that columns like 'payer_account_id' are exposed as 'payerAccountId' in the raw model object, and if CryptoAllowanceViewModel inherits this via BaseAllowanceViewModel without explicit field filtering, does this leak the payer account relationship that could be used to redirect staking reward payments?

[File: 'File Name: rest/controllers/cryptoAllowanceController.js -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: extractCryptoAllowancesQuery()] Can an unprivileged external user send a request where the spender.id filter uses the 'lte' operator with value 0, causing the query to include a condition 'spender <= $2' with value 0, which combined with the amount > 0 filter still returns allowances for spender account 0 (system account) that may have special permissions to access staking reward funds?

[File: 'File Name: rest/controllers/cryptoAllowanceController.js -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: getAccountCryptoAllowances()] Can an unprivileged external user use the timestamp.to field (which is null for active allowances, per nsToSecNs returning null for undefined) to distinguish currently active allowances from expired ones on a staking reward account, focusing their theft attempt on accounts with active allowances that still have full spending authority over unpaid staking rewards?

[File: 'File Name: rest/controllers/cryptoAllowanceController.js -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: getAccountCryptoAllowances()] Can an unprivileged external user perform a timing attack by repeatedly querying /accounts/:id/allowances/crypto for a staking account at high frequency, observing changes in the amount field over time to detect when staking rewards are credited to the account, then immediately using a known allowance to drain the newly credited rewards?
